summary: Excellent clean soundboard capture of a peak‑1977 show with smoking second‑set jams and flawless recording quality
review: The consensus is that this June‑1977 performance is one of the band’s finest – tight grooves, towering Jerry solos (a near‑90‑minute run), and a second set packed with Estimated, Eyes of the World, The Other One, Wharf Rat and a blistering Brokedown Palace.  Charlie Miller’s unpatched soundboard source is described as crystal‑clear, with an especially transparent AUD patch, making the recording sound almost like being onstage.

ai_rating.rationale: All reviews praise both the musical performance and the audio fidelity; multiple reviewers call it a top‑10/77 recording with an “excellent” soundboard, yielding a near‑perfect combined score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clean, almost unpatched soundboard; transparent AUD keyboard patch; crisp and clear throughout with no noticeable dropouts or muddiness.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set is described as tight and seamless – the first set flows smoothly into an even tighter second set, highlighted by a 90‑minute Jerry marathon with only a four‑minute drum break. Energy crackles from start to finish.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as ‘man possessed’, delivering a near‑hour‑plus solo run; never stops and drives the show’s intensity.
band_member_comments.Phil: Appreciated for his bass foundation (no specific critique, just general praise).
band_member_comments.Keys: Keyboard sound praised – AUD patch is “almost completely transparent”, making the keys sit perfectly in the mix.
band_member_comments.Drums: Solid, with a short drum break that keeps momentum; the mix captures the drums cleanly.
